entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's platform for hiring and career services; must implement contextual matching

entities.name: Trade-off-based hiring
entities.context: Viewing hiring as navigating trade-offs between bundles of skills rather than stack-ranking candidates

entities.name: Contextual Matching
entities.context: Visualization approach that prompts recruiters to define acceptable trade-offs rather than searching for unicorns

entities.name: Comparative advantage
entities.context: Economics principle that capability is about how your specific mix complements the existing system, not being best at everything

entities.name: Stack-ranking
entities.context: Traditional approach of ranking candidates linearly; critiqued as flattening multidimensional human capability

entities.name: ATS
entities.context: Applicant Tracking System using scalar metrics (1-10) that leads to averaging rather than optimizing

entities.name: Multidimensional capability
entities.context: Belief that human capability is multidimensional and non-linear, not reducible to scalar metrics

relationships.context: Platform must move toward contextual matching visualizations
Hiring philosophy mirrors the economics principle of comparative advantage
Contextual matching replaces and opposes traditional stack-ranking approach
Traditional ATS logic enables stack-ranking through scalar metrics
Trade-off approach acknowledges that human capability is multidimensional
